channel.title: World - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: U.K. Prime Minister Starmer to resign as Labour Party seeks reboot - The Washington Post
item.description:
1. U.K. Prime Minister Starmer to resign as Labour Party seeks reboot  The Washington Post
  2. Starmer Announces Resignation; Burnham Wins Key Endorsement  The New York Times
  3. Keir Starmer’s resignation speech: anatomy of a picture  The Guardian
  4. The Man Who Couldn’t Do It  The Atlantic
  5. U.K. will get its seventh prime minister in 10 years. Here’s how they fell.  The Washington Post

item.title: US partially lifts Iran oil sanctions amid ‘encouraging’ talks - Al Jazeera
item.description:
1. US partially lifts Iran oil sanctions amid ‘encouraging’ talks  Al Jazeera
  2. Iran Live Updates: U.S. Eases Oil Sanctions as Vance Hails Talks as a “Good Foundation” for Peace  The New York Times
  3. Iran wins sanctions relief as U.S. reports progress in peace talks  Honolulu Star-Advertiser
  4. Treasury Department authorizes Iranian oil sales through August  CNBC
  5. Trump’s Iran agreement embraces sanctions relief, a policy he and his team once denounced  CNN

item.title: 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3 - NPR
item.description:
1. 2 students in custody after shooting at high school in Philippines kills 3  NPR

  4. Teenagers arrested after three children killed in school shooting  The Telegraph
  5. Philippines: School shooting leaves three dead, seven wounded  BBC

item.title: No Gas, No Summer Camps, Sporadic Power: Ukraine Escalates Crimea Attacks - The New York Times
item.description:

item.title: Iran-US peace talks show ‘encouraging progress,’ say mediators - politico.eu
item.description:
1. Iran-US peace talks show ‘encouraging progress,’ say mediators  politico.eu

item.title: At least 18 dead in France, including two children in hot car, as Europe bakes - Reuters
item.description:
1. At least 18 dead in France, including two children in hot car, as Europe bakes  Reuters

  3. Nation Introduces Alcohol Ban Amid Deadly High Temperatures  The Daily Beast
  4. Temperatures to exceed 40C in European heat wave as casualties mount  The Globe and Mail
  5. There won't be many places hotter than France this week  Yahoo

item.title: ‘Albania is not for sale’: Protests grow over Kushner-linked luxury development project - CNBC

item.title: UK PM Keir Starmer resigns. And, US and Iran agree to roadmap for final deal - NPR
item.description:

item.title: Record stash of cocaine found buried underground in Australia - CBS News
item.description:

item.title: Shipping stalls in Strait of Hormuz after Iran declares key waterway closed again - CNBC
item.description:
1. Shipping stalls in Strait of Hormuz after Iran declares key waterway closed again  CNBC

item.title: Andy Burnham on course to be British PM in weeks after Keir Starmer quits - politico.eu
item.description:
1. Andy Burnham on course to be British PM in weeks after Keir Starmer quits  politico.eu

  3. The Most Important Labour Leader in a Generation  The American Prospect
  4. The politician who kicked his way to power  Politico
  5. After Andy Burnham’s Win, the UK Is Set to Test How Far Charisma Can Shift Electoral Dynamics  The New York Times
